name: BOM Digest
summary: Hyper-local Aussie weather alerts for tradies, parents, event planners, construction sites. BOM data is free — the UX and delivery is the product. Tiers from $5–$49/mo. Moat is audience + tuned alert logic.

name: Figma Comment Digest
summary: Morning rollup of Figma comments across all projects, sorted by file and urgency. Sent to email or Slack. Designers constantly miss client feedback buried in notification floods.

name: Calendly Conflict Guard
summary: Detects when a Calendly booking lands on an existing calendar event. Fires an instant alert + 1-click reschedule link. Dead simple, real pain point for solo consultants.

name: Travel Packing App
summary: Gap in market: smart quantity rules (1 pair socks/day, jeans every 3 days), weather + activity combined, real family group packing, bag weight/space logic, habit learning over time. MVP opportunity: smart quantity calculator. Research confirms: collaborative packing (couples/families sharing real-time lists) is the clearest gap — no dominant player. Also: itinerary-aware AI lists (TripIt integration) is most-requested feature. Minimalist/one-bag niche (r/HerOneBag) vocal and underserved. Most users default to Trello/Excel — no killer feature yet.

name: Signature Stylizer
summary: Upload your existing handwritten signature, get back 3-5 stylised/calligraphic versions that still look like yours. Sell each for $2-5. Gap confirmed: no one does upload-and-stylise well. Artlogo charges $19 with human calligraphers. AI tools all fail at identity preservation. Smart path: start with humans, train model on your own orders. Vector-path approach more reliable than diffusion models for maintaining signature identity.

name: Woolworths Shopping Assistant
summary: AI assistant that manages Woolworths online orders. Tracks purchase history and frequency, accepts additions via iMessage or Telegram, auto-builds cart and checks out. Browser automation confirmed working — can search, add to cart. Needs logged-in account for checkout. Could expand to other grocers.

name: PDF Form Filler
summary: Upload any old-school PDF form (doctor, government, insurance, real estate), AI reads the fields, fills everything from your saved profile — one and done. Vision AI handles non-fillable flat PDFs. Free tier 1 form/month, Pro $5-9/mo unlimited. B2B angle: clinics pre-fill for patients. No competitor nails this specific use case.

name: InspectionIQ
note: Petar received a RedBookInspect report this morning — said this is exactly what he's dealing with
summary: Upload any pre-purchase inspection report (car or property), get a plain-English summary with a risk score, estimated repair costs, and a buy / negotiate / walk away recommendation. Buyers hate reading these reports.

name: TimezoneSafe
note: Petar had a 6:30 AM Teams call this morning — said this is exactly what he's dealing with
summary: Calendar assistant that flags when a meeting is brutal for one participant (e.g. 6:30 AM) and suggests 3 alternative slots that work for everyone. Pitched at remote teams booking across AU/UK/US.

name: FounderForecast
summary: Lightweight churn prediction for indie SaaS. Connect Stripe + one analytics source, get a weekly digest: these 3 customers are about to leave and here is why. Enterprise tools do this — no one has made it dead simple for solo founders.
